title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: Concise findings: - `scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` and `scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh` are in parity across `agent-orchestration` and `agent-feed-runner` (identical wrappers), and both repos’ prompt tests pass locally. - High-risk portability gap: all four wrappers hardcode `ENGINE="/Users/masayuki/Dev/kernel-orchestration-tools/scripts/check-codex-prompt-contract.sh"` with no fallback. This will break on non-matching hosts/CI runners. - Workflow parity is **not** maintained: `.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml` has material divergence (different path filters, static-check lists, and gate/test stages). Shared prompt-contract coverage is therefore inconsistent across repos. - Missing workflow update for shared-engine drift: neither repo’s gate can be triggered by changes inside `kernel-orchestration-tools` (external shared engine), so engine regressions can bypass repo-local CI triggers. - Missing explicit regression guard: no dedicated test/workflow step validates wrapper engine resolution behavior (absolute-path dependency/fallback policy), so breakage mode is only detected implicitly at runtime.
